blockingIssues.description: X/Twitter adapter is a stub and does not implement real twitter-api-v2 operations (tweet/reply/retweet/like/search), so required first-party adapter behavior is missing.
Slack adapter is a stub and does not initialize/use @slack/bolt Socket Mode or perform real API operations for declared tools.
Email adapter is a stub and does not perform real SMTP/IMAP operations via nodemailer/imap-simple for send/list/read/reply flows.

PlatformToolsManager is instantiated in runtime but not wired to integration lifecycle events, so dynamic platform-tool registration/deregistration is not actually triggered on integration load/unload.
McpHub.removeTool(name) only mutates dynamic map and does not remove tool handles from active sessions, leaving stale tools callable after deregistration.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify milestone m2 acceptance criteria that first-party integrations labeled implemented (X/Twitter, Slack, Email) must execute real provider operations, not placeholder/stub responses.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review f3 found three adapters with explicit placeholder behavior while still passing local tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align feature skillName metadata with actually available skills (or provide compatibility alias) so workers can reliably invoke required skills without procedural deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both reviewed features reference skillName=worker while transcripts report skill invocation failure for 'worker'.

rejectedObservations.observation: Reviewer prompt references .factory/library/architecture.md but repository currently lacks that file.
Skill mismatch ('worker' unavailable) appears in both reviews.
